articles.title: Wave of US attacks target Iran for 12th night in a row, two killed in Shalamcheh
articles.summary: Wave of US attacks target Iran for 12th night in a row, two killed in Shalamcheh A US attack on Iran’s Shalamcheh border crossing with Iraq killed at least two and wounded 11 others on Thursday, state media reported. The strike is part of a wave of assaults targeting sites across the country for the 12th consecutive night.  A US attack on Iran’s Shalamcheh border crossing with Iraq killed at least two and wounded 11 others on Thursday, local media reported. About 1,300 Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa Mosque compound
articles.summary: About 1,300 Israeli settlers storm Al-Aqsa Mosque compound Approximately 1,300 Israeli settlers, accompanied by Israeli forces, stormed the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound early on Thursday, the Jerusalem Governorate reported.  The storming took place on Tisha B'Av, a holiday which commemorates the destruction of the two Jewish temples and the exile of Jewish people. Israeli forces imposed tight restrictions Jerusalem’s Old City, preventing Palestinians from entering the mosque and restricting them from its surroundings.

articles.summary: US attacks farmers' properties in Iran's Bushehr Equipments belonging to farmers in Bushehr's Dashti county were struck by US missiles, Iranian state media reported.  According to the Bushehr Deputy Governor, there were no casualties in the attack, with the extent of damage in the area currently under investigation.

IRGC claims several attacks on US army facilities in Kuwait
articles.summary: IRGC claims several attacks on US army facilities in Kuwait The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced attacks targeting US military infrastructure in Kuwait.  In a statement, the IRGC said it targeted a large warehouse of military equipment, a Patriot missile defense system, and a hangar of American MQ9 drones at the Ali al-Salem Air Base.  It also carried out strikes on two helicopter hangars in Camp al-Adiri as well as targeted a telecommunications tower in the Gulf country.

articles.summary: Attacks on Iran will be met with a 'powerful and decisive' response, Aragchi warns Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi warned that any attacks on his country will be met with a "powerful and decisive" response. "Our defense doctrine is clear: eye for an eye," he said in a post on X. "Any aggression against Iran, including our infrastructure, will compel a powerful and decisive response." articles.title: US warns citizens in the Middle East to 'exercise caution'
articles.summary: US warns citizens in the Middle East to 'exercise caution' The US Department of State has urged citizens to "exercise caution and heightened vigilance" amid the escalating conflict in the region.  "Worldwide Caution: Due to heightened tensions in the Middle East, the security environment remains complex with the potential for unforeseen escalation," the statement read. It instructed Americans to be prepared for "flight cancellations, periodic airspace closures, and potential travel disruptions", and cautioned that US facilities across the world may be targeted.

articles.title: US passes $1 trillion military spending bill
articles.summary: US passes $1 trillion military spending bill The US House of Representatives narrowly passed its version of a massive defence policy bill on Wednesday, despite Democrats' concerns about its huge price tag, the Iran war and a provision that would boost Pentagon ties to Israel. The fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act, or NDAA, authorises an unprecedented $1.15 trillion in military spending and passed the House by a vote of 216 to 212. The vote was largely along party lines, as all but seven Republicans voted in favour of the measure and all but six Democrats voted against. The NDAA ha

articles.title: US strike on Iran's border with Iraq kills two
articles.summary: US strike on Iran's border with Iraq kills two A US attack on Iran’s Shalamcheh border crossing with Iraq killed at least two and wounded 11 others on Thursday, state news agency IRNA reported.  Meanwhile, a US missile hit Bushehr for a second time this morning. According to IRNA, no casualties were reported in these attacks and the extent of the damage is under investigation.  Another American missile attack was reported in Andimeshk, located in the country's Khuzestan province. Valiollah Hayati, deputy governor for security and law enforcement in southwestern Kh

articles.title: Vessel catches fire after passing through mined route in Hormuz Strait, IRGC says
articles.summary: Vessel catches fire after passing through mined route in Hormuz Strait, IRGC says Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said that one of three oil tankers were set ablaze after a blast while attempting to pass through a mined route south of the Strait of Hormuz. According to the statement, the two other tankers turned back following this incident. "The Strait of Hormuz is under our control," the IRGC said, adding that as long as the US continues "its evil deeds" in the region, the waterway will remain closed.

articles.title: Fire onboard Saudi vessel hit in Red Sea
articles.summary: Fire onboard Saudi vessel hit in Red Sea  A Saudi-owned vessel was hit while sailing in the Red Sea, causing the front of the ship to be set on ifire, Saudi state news agency SPA reported citing an official source at the General Transport Authority. According to the source, all crew members onboard the Encelia were safe. Authorities were reportedly taking key measures to secure the vessel and crew as well as protect the marine environment.

articles.title: Morocco records 310 forest fires since January, with 1,850 hectares burned
articles.summary: Morocco recorded 310 forest fires between Jan. 1 and July 20, destroying more than 1,850 hectares nationwide, according to the National Agency of Water and Forests (ANEF). Although the number of fires increased by nearly 32% compared with the 10-year average for the same period, the total area burned declined by about 30%, falling from […] The post Morocco records 310 forest fires since January, with 1,850 hectares burned appeared first on HESPRESS English - Morocco News .
